_meta.model: facebook/convnext-tiny-224 (ALL findings from this model only)
_meta.epistemics_warning: ⚠️ All findings from rigorous 3-run ablation. However, still 1 model, 1 device. CPU behavior can differ significantly between x86 and ARM (Oryon). Check architecture before applying rules.

findings.title: opset 19+ causes severe regression on CPU EP (3-4x slowdown)
findings.observation: opset 17: p50=43.7ms. opset 19: p50=160ms (3.7x). opset 20: p50=131ms (3.0x). opset 21: p50=170ms (3.9x). opset 22: p50=85ms (1.9x). All runs consistent — not noise.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ORT C++ Transpose Optimizer has kMaxSupportedOpset gate. If model opset > kMaxSupportedOpset, the entire Transpose Optimizer is skipped silently. ConvNext has 42 Transpose nodes — without optimization, each executes as a full memory-layout copy. Code: onnxruntime/core/optimizer/transpose_optimization/optimizer_api.h. kMaxSupportedOpset is bumped with each ORT release.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: For CPU EP: default to opset 17. Do NOT try opset 19+ unless you first verify that the shipping ORT version's kMaxSupportedOpset >= target_opset.
findings.confidence: high — mechanism confirmed by source code + ORT session opt-level experiment (ENABLE_ALL removes the regression)
findings.scope: Models with many Transpose nodes (ConvNext, ViT, vision transformers). Models with few Transposes (BERT) may be less affected.
findings.do_not_generalize_to: QNN NPU EP or DML EP — kMaxSupportedOpset is a CPU-only ORT optimizer gate. These EPs have their own kernel dispatch unaffected by this.

findings.title: matmul_add_fusion is a CONFIRMED REGRESSION on ConvNext CPU (+38ms, ~87%)
findings.observation: matmul_add_fusion: p50=81.7ms, runs=[63.0, 70.8, 111.2ms]. Baseline p50=43.7ms. All 3 runs far above highest baseline run (45.4ms).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ORT baseline already converts MatMul+Add→Gemm (37 Gemm in model.onnx). Applying matmul_add_fusion on top may create redundant kernel dispatch or conflicting operator mapping. Requires profiling to confirm.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT apply matmul_add_fusion for CPU EP on models where baseline already uses Gemm (check model.onnx for Gemm nodes before applying this pass).
findings.confidence: high — 3 independent runs, all far above baseline; direction is unambiguous
findings.scope: ConvNext and models where ORT L2 baseline already fuses MatMul+Add→Gemm
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Models where baseline does NOT have Gemm (the pass may legitimately help there)

findings.title: transpose_optimizer is neutral on ConvNext CPU (NOT +270ms as previously reported)
findings.observation: winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50): 42.3 / 52.3 / 41.8ms — overlapping baseline. Earlier winml eval-based measurement showed +270ms — this was a measurement artifact.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: winml eval includes HF preprocessing + model load + no warmup. The +270ms was preprocessing overhead, not inference regression. Pure inference measurement (winml perf) shows no effect.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: transpose_optimizer is neutral for ConvNext CPU — neither helpful nor harmful. Can be omitted from search space.
findings.confidence: high — measurement methodology confirmed; tool comparison validated
findings.falsified_by: Earlier winml eval measurement — RETRACTED. Use winml perf for all latency comparisons.
findings.measurement_lesson: Always use winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50) for latency experiments. Never use winml eval latency to compare configs.

findings.title: nchwc_transformer is neutral on ConvNext CPU
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: NCHWc SIMD layout benefits Conv-heavy models. ConvNext has 22 Conv nodes but 57.7% of kernel time is Gemm. The bottleneck is not memory layout but compute throughput — NCHWc doesn't help.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: nchwc_transformer is low-priority for ConvNext-class models. Profile first — if Conv% > 40%, try nchwc. If Gemm% > 50%, skip.
findings.confidence: medium — 3 runs, neutral result; mechanism is a hypothesis

findings.title: Baseline (no extra flags) is the optimal config for ConvNext CPU
findings.observation: No flag in 22-experiment ablation improved p50 beyond noise. Baseline p50=43.7ms is the floor.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: ORT L2 baseline already applies gelu_fusion and MatMul→Gemm before any user flags. The effective optimization space is narrow for ConvNext on CPU. Compute bottleneck (Gemm=57.7%) is not addressable via graph passes.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: For CPU EP on ConvNext-class models: skip optimization pass sweep. Go directly to quantization experiments.
findings.do_not_generalize_to: BERT/Transformer models where attention_fusion + skip_layer_norm can significantly help

findings.title: CPU EP opset 21 is 3.9x SLOWER — opposite of QNN NPU behavior
findings.observation: CPU opset 21: p50=170ms. CPU opset 17: p50=43.7ms. QNN NPU opset 21: p50=8.45ms (2.3x FASTER).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: Same kMaxSupportedOpset gate as cpu-001. CPU and QNN NPU have completely different optimizer paths. CPU regression from Transpose Optimizer bypass. QNN NPU speedup from better kernel dispatch (mechanism under research).
findings.action_for_autoconfig: EP ISOLATION: CPU opset findings MUST NOT influence QNN NPU search space, and vice versa. Always validate per EP independently.
findings.scope: ALL — this is a meta-rule about EP isolation, not model-specific

skip: 19, 20, 21, 22 — kMaxSupportedOpset regression (cpu-001). Only safe to try if ORT version's kMaxSupportedOpset >= target.
opset.dialectical_note: ⚠️ This rule is ORT-version dependent. Check kMaxSupportedOpset for the shipping ORT build before skipping higher opsets.

quantization.recommended: w8a8 (CPU benefits most from small model size)
quantization.dialectical_note: ⚠️ W8A8 on CPU not yet validated for ConvNext. General guidance — run accuracy gate.

compile.dialectical_note: ⚠️ winml compile targets QNN EPContext. Not applicable to CPU EP.

skip: matmul_add_fusion if model already has Gemm (cpu-002)
graph_passes.dialectical_note: ⚠️ Skip rules are Gemm-bottleneck specific. Conv-heavy models may still benefit from nchwc_transformer.

meta_lessons.measurement_discipline: Always use winml perf (warmup=10, iter=50) for latency. Never use winml eval latency. See cpu-003.
meta_lessons.ep_isolation: CPU findings (especially opset regression) DO NOT transfer to QNN NPU or DML. Each EP has its own optimizer path. See cpu-006.
meta_lessons.baseline_check: Before applying any fusion flag, check model.onnx for existing fused ops. If Gemm already present, matmul_add_fusion is likely a no-op or regression.
